bullets.text: Q1 combined ratio of 88.8 with 16% NPW growth; Commercial Lines led with 26% NPW growth and an extraordinary CR of 83.6; Property CR of 97.9 (about 11 points worse without aggregate stop-loss reinsurance protection).
Companywide NPW grew 16% with all product lines contributing; investment portfolio returned 3.2% in Q1, the best quarterly gain since 2009, with equities returning 13.3%.

Agency channel represents majority of Personal and Commercial Lines policies; Robinson new business through agents grew 40% in Q1 vs. Q1 2018; Progressive ranked top personal lines carrier in J.D. Power 2019 Independent Agent Study.

Signals of softening personal auto market noted; Progressive holds #1 position by written premium in independent agency channel for both personal and commercial auto; only ~2% of bundled market penetrated, indicating long runway.

Investment portfolio returned 3.2% in Q1; $156 million federal renewable energy tax credit benefit reversed and $24 million investment written down after sponsor alleged fraud in solar tax credit funds.

Business Owners Policy (BOP) development well advanced; Portfolio quoting system designed directly with agents via focus groups to enable bundled quotes across auto, home, special lines, and commercial lines.

Personal auto market softening observed; Progressive committed to efficient marketing spend and surgical cost focus while monitoring trends at state, consumer segment, and product level to maintain competitive rates.
